# ARC-QUIC-CRT-0030 - QUIC CRT client-side ClientHello PSK-attempt architecture

## Purpose

Describe the smallest honest PSK-attempt seam: the managed client/runtime path consumes dormant detached resumption material to build a PSK-capable ClientHello with binder emission while keeping early data closed and without claiming resumed-handshake success yet.

## Scope

This slice covers only client-side ClientHello PSK-attempt construction from the dormant detached carrier and the minimum parser acceptance needed to ingest the emitted PSK-attempt message. It does not add resumed-handshake success, 0-RTT packet support, anti-replay, key update, or a public API expansion.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0132

## Design Summary

The managed client/runtime path already owns opaque ticket ingress, a runtime-local ticket snapshot, a detached carrier handoff, and the minimum resumption credential material. This slice consumes that dormant carrier to construct a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt with the supported `psk_dhe_ke` mode, a single PSK identity, a computed binder, and an explicitly closed early-data gate. The carrier stays immutable and dormant outside the emitted ClientHello, and the slice does not claim resumed-handshake success or any public surface change.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicDetachedResumptionTicketSnapshot.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/QuicPostHandshakeTicketTestSupport.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0132.cs

## Risks

- If the binder is emitted without the dormant carrier actually feeding the ClientHello attempt path, the slice would look implemented while remaining dead code.
- If the detached carrier is consumed without preserving the closed early-data gate or the bootstrap fallback, the slice would overclaim resumption capability that the repository has not yet proven.
- If the pre_shared_key ordering or binder-length math is wrong, the server parser will reject the ClientHello and the attempt path will not be truthful.
- If the carrier leaks into public API or becomes part of the supported IsSupported promise, the slice will widen beyond the narrow proof floor.
